cells.label: active
cells.function: active voice — meN- prefix (place-assimilating); the agent is subject

cells.label: passive
cells.function: passive voice — di- prefix; the patient is subject

conditioning: meN- assimilates to the stem's initial consonant place (mem-/men-/meng-/meny-); di- is invariant

examples: active meN- is unmarked in the English pivot (it carries no English feature)
fit_none.note: the passive di- is recoverable because English passive marks Voice=Pass; the active meN- has no English-feature correlate, so a pivot-based detector recovers only the passive — the expected gap

evidence_citations.stat: case absent → voice-focus is the Austronesian alternative

prose: ind has a VOICE system: active meN- (place-assimilating: mem-/men-/meng-/meny-) vs passive di-. The passive di- is recovered from data (it predicts the English Voice=Pass feature, lift ~10). The active meN- is unmarked in English and so is not recoverable from the pivot — it is the inferred default alternant, not an evidenced cell. Recommendation: PROMOTE passive di-; flag active meN- as inferred (needs a vernacular-internal alternation signal, not the English pivot).
